normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
inductive Tree : Type
| Nil : Tree
| Node : Tree → Int → Tree → Tree
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the same immutable binary search tree datatype as in the Rust code (Nil or Node(left, key, right)).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def bstInsert : Tree → Int → Tree
| Tree.Nil, key => Tree.Node Tree.Nil key Tree.Nil
| Tree.Node left k right, key =>
    if key < k then Tree.Node (bstInsert left key) k right
    else if key > k then Tree.Node left k (bstInsert right key)
    else Tree.Node left k right
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: State the Lean definition of bst insertion matching the Rust pattern match and the three-way comparison (less/greater/no-op on equality).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem bstInsert_terminates (t : Tree) (key : Int) : True := by trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The recursive definition is structurally recursive on the input tree (so it terminates). (In Lean, this is typically discharged by the termination checker; here we record the obligation as a trivial placeholder.)

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def BST : Tree → Prop
| Tree.Nil => True
| Tree.Node l k r => BST l ∧ BST r ∧ (∀ x, x ∈ keys l → x < k) ∧ (∀ x, x ∈ keys r → k < x)

-- where keys collects all stored keys

def keys : Tree → Set Int
| Tree.Nil => ∅
| Tree.Node l k r => keys l ∪ {k} ∪ keys r

theorem bstInsert_preserves_BST : ∀ t key, BST t → BST (bstInsert t key)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree satisfies the binary search tree ordering invariant, then inserting a key preserves that invariant.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bstInsert_eq (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  (∃ l r, t = Tree.Node l k r) → bstInsert t k = t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the tree already contains a node with the same key at the root, insertion is a no-op (matches the `else` branch when `key == k`).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mem_keys_insert : ∀ t key, key ∈ keys (bstInsert t key) := by
  intro t key
  -- proof by induction on t following the Rust cases
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: After insertion, the inserted key is present in the resulting tree.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mem_keys_other : ∀ t key x,
  x ≠ key → (x ∈ keys t ↔ x ∈ keys (bstInsert t key)) := by
  intro t key x hx
  -- proof by induction on t following the Rust comparisons
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: For any key x different from the inserted key, insertion does not remove it; it remains present iff it was present before.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insert_only_changes_one_branch : ∀ t key,
  match t with
  | Tree.Nil => True
  | Tree.Node l k r =>
      (key < k → bstInsert t key = Tree.Node (bstInsert l key) k r) ∧
      (key > k → bstInsert t key = Tree.Node l k (bstInsert r key)) ∧
      (key = k → bstInsert t key = Tree.Node l k r)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The implementation only recurses into the left subtree when `key < k`, into the right subtree when `key > k`, and otherwise leaves the node unchanged.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bstInsert_correct : ∀ t key,
  keys (bstInsert t key) = keys t ∪ {key} := by
  intro t key
  -- proof by induction on t using the three-way comparison
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The set of keys in the resulting tree equals the original set plus the inserted key (duplicates do not add new nodes).
